answers: Gratitude is the practice of recognizing and appreciating the good things in your life, no matter how small. It shifts your focus from what's lacking to what you already have.
Gratitude practice korte protidin 3 ta jinish lekho jar jonno tumi grateful. Eta choto choto jinish hote pare — ektu roddur, ekta bhalo khabar, ba karo hasi.
Research shows gratitude improves mental health, strengthens relationships, enhances sleep quality, and boosts overall happiness. Grateful people tend to be more resilient and optimistic.
A gratitude journal is where you write down things you're thankful for each day. Even on tough days, finding something to appreciate rewires your brain toward positivity over time.
